novel_features.name: Coverage-list 8-K item pivot
novel_features.description: Across a saved watchlist of CIKs, surface 8-Ks filed in the window grouped by Item code (2.05 restructuring, 5.02 exec change, 4.02 non-reliance).
novel_features.rationale: Joins the local filings table with an in-CLI watchlist file. No single SEC endpoint does cross-CIK + Item-code pivot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Equity analysts triage filings by Item code, not by company. Pick this when an agent needs cross-company 8-K filtering by Item.

novel_features.name: Insider cluster detector
novel_features.description: Flag issuers where 3+ distinct insiders filed Form 4 with the same transaction code (open-market buy or sale) within a rolling N-day window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a SQL self-join over the local insider_txns table grouped by issuer with HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT insider). Form 4 endpoints give the rows; the cluster is the JOIN.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Clustered insider selling is a textbook forensic signal. Pick this when an agent needs derived signals over raw Form 4 data.

novel_features.name: Live filing watch with filters
novel_features.description: Stream the SEC Atom getcurrent feed with multi-dimensional filtering: form type, 8-K item, CIK watchlist, and keyword regex. Emits one NDJSON line per match.
novel_features.rationale: sec-api offers a paid raw filing stream; no free competitor combines multi-dimensional filters with structured output. Polls Atom feed locally; verify-safe via PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY env-var.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents that monitor for specific filing events should subscribe to this stream rather than polling submissions per CIK.

novel_features.name: XBRL peer-group bench
novel_features.description: Take an XBRL concept for one reporting period, group by SIC code, and emit percentile statistics (p10/p50/p90) across every public-company filer in that SIC.
novel_features.rationale: JOIN xbrl_facts ON companies.sic; compute percentiles in-process. XBRL frames feeds the rows but offers no SIC grouping. Competitors stop at the raw frame call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Quant researchers and equity analysts need peer-group benchmarks. Pick this when an agent should answer 'how does X compare to its industry peers'.

novel_features.name: Cross-section concept across companies
novel_features.description: Pivot a single XBRL concept across an explicit company list and the last N reporting periods. Output as a wide pivot table (one row per company, one column per period).
novel_features.rationale: SELECT cik, period, value FROM xbrl_facts WHERE tag=? AND cik IN(?) ORDER BY cik, period. edgartools covers one company × all periods; frames covers all companies × one period; neither does both axes constrained at once.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Comp-set analysis is one of the highest-frequency analyst rituals. Pick this when an agent compares fundamentals across an explicit ticker list.

novel_features.name: Restatement / non-reliance scanner
novel_features.description: Surface 8-K Item 4.02 (non-reliance on prior financials) plus all 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments filed in the window — the textbook accounting-irregularity signal.
novel_features.rationale: SELECT WHERE (form='8-K' AND items LIKE '%4.02%') OR form LIKE '%/A' AND filed >= cursor. No competitor surfaces this as a first-class command — it requires knowing the Item code + the amendment-form list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Forensic accounting agents look for restatements as a leading indicator of trouble. Pick this when an agent needs accounting-quality signals.

novel_features.name: Late filer scanner
novel_features.description: Find issuers that filed an NT 10-K, NT 10-Q, or NT 20-F in the window — the SEC's 'I'm going to miss my reporting deadline' notification.
novel_features.rationale: SELECT WHERE form IN ('NT 10-K','NT 10-Q','NT 20-F') AND filed >= cursor; JOIN companies for name+SIC. bellingcat/EDGAR can search but no competitor surfaces NT-form as first-class.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Late filings are leading indicators of operational or accounting trouble. Pick this when an agent needs to flag at-risk companies.

novel_features.name: 13F holdings delta
novel_features.description: Diff an institutional investor's 13F holdings across two consecutive quarters. Categorize each issuer as ADD / EXIT / INCREASE / DECREASE with share-count delta.
novel_features.rationale: LEFT JOIN holdings_13f current vs prior on (filer, issuer). sec-api/edgartools require hand-rolled diff in Python; no competing tool does it as a single command.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tracking institutional money flow is a classic factor signal. Pick this when an agent compares a fund's positioning across quarters.

narrative.headline: Every SEC filing, every XBRL fact, every insider trade — synced into a local SQLite store you can pivot, search, and watch offline.
narrative.value_prop: An agent-native CLI for the entire SEC EDGAR surface — data.sec.gov XBRL, efts.sec.gov full-text search, and the live Atom feed. The synced SQLite store enables joins no single SEC endpoint supports: insider-cluster detection across issuers, XBRL peer-group benchmarks by SIC, 13F holdings deltas across quarters, and live filing watches with multi-dimensional filters. All free — SEC provides no API key, just a mandatory User-Agent header.
narrative.auth_narrative: SEC EDGAR requires no API key. Every request must include a User-Agent header naming a human and contact email (e.g. 'Alex Researcher alex@example.com'). Set SEC_EDGAR_USER_AGENT in your environment; the CLI refuses to run without it. The SEC enforces a 10-req/sec rate cap per host; this CLI caps itself at 8 req/sec with jitter.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the User-Agent is set and SEC hosts are reachable.
Resolve a ticker to its CIK from the local company-ticker map.
Apple's last five 10-Ks from the synced submissions store.
Apple's last four quarters of income-statement XBRL facts.
Flag issuers with 3+ insiders selling in any 5-day window over the last 30 days.
Stream live filings matching multi-dim filters as NDJSON.

troubleshoots.fix: The User-Agent header is missing or generic. Set SEC_EDGAR_USER_AGENT='Your Name your@email.com' and retry.
Reduce concurrency. The CLI caps at 8 req/sec by default; --concurrency 4 halves it. Sync commands resume from cursor.
CIKs are 10 digits with leading zeros. Use 'companies lookup <ticker>' to resolve; the CLI also accepts un-padded CIKs and pads them.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty 'facts company' output for a known filer
troubleshoots.fix: Not all filers report XBRL (e.g. older filings, foreign private issuers using 20-F). 'filings list' shows what they did file.

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when an agent needs to query SEC filings, XBRL financial facts, insider trading (Form 4), or institutional holdings (13F) across many companies or many periods — anything that benefits from a local SQLite store rather than a per-call API roundtrip. For one-off single-filing reads, the typed endpoint mirrors (filings get, facts get) work without sync. For cross-company pivots, insider clustering, peer-group benchmarks, or live filing watches, the transcendence commands turn what would be ten API calls + a Python loop into one CLI invocation.

recipes.title: Apple income-statement trend, agent-friendly
recipes.explanation: XBRL responses are deeply nested; --select with dotted paths picks only the three concepts the agent needs across the last 8 periods.

recipes.title: Cluster-buying watchlist scan
recipes.explanation: Open-market insider buying (Form 4 code P) clustered across 3+ insiders in 14 days, last 90 days. The store-backed JOIN is what makes this one command.

recipes.title: Quarterly comp-set revenue pivot
recipes.explanation: Apple vs Microsoft vs Alphabet revenue, last 8 quarters, as a single pivot. Both axes constrained — neither edgartools nor the frames endpoint covers this shape.

recipes.title: Watchlist Monday triage
recipes.explanation: Cross-CIK 8-K Item pivot — Maya's exact Monday ritual. The watchlist file is one CIK per line.

recipes.title: Late-filer compliance scan
recipes.explanation: Surfaces NT 10-K filings (companies that warned they'd miss their reporting deadline). Single SELECT against the local filings table.

trigger_phrases: find restatements in the last quarter
